title: dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. anatolicum
intro: Dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. anatolicum, also known as the Anatolian Dorycnium, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant and a diuretic. The Anatolian Dorycnium is believed to have various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as helping to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
